title: OpenAI board removes and reinstates Sam Altman
domain_context: OpenAI is structured as a non-profit organization that wholly controls a capped-profit commercial subsidiary, giving its board legal authority over the CEO independent of commercial investors like Microsoft.
description: OpenAI's non-profit board removed CEO Sam Altman without prior notice to major investors including Microsoft. Employees threatened mass resignation, and Altman was reinstated five days later with a restructured board.

title: DeepSeek releases the R1 reasoning model
domain_context: Frontier AI lab valuations and GPU demand forecasts have largely been priced on the assumption that state-of-the-art model performance requires proportionally massive compute and training budgets.
description: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek released the R1 reasoning model, reporting a training cost well below comparable US frontier models and built on export-restricted hardware. US technology equities, including NVIDIA, saw a sharp single-day decline following the release.

title: XcodeGhost malware found in App Store apps
domain_context: Apple's App Store review process screens compiled app binaries for malicious behavior but does not independently verify the developer toolchain (Xcode) used to compile them.
description: A modified version of Apple's Xcode developer tool, distributed through unofficial mirrors in China, was found to inject malware into any app compiled with it. Over 40 apps distributed through the App Store, including widely used consumer apps, were affected before Apple removed them.

title: Apple launches Vision Pro
domain_context: Apple's hardware platforms typically rely on third-party developers and major content publishers building native apps early in a product's life to drive mainstream adoption.
description: Apple launched its Vision Pro spatial computing headset at a $3,500 price point. Several major streaming and media companies declined to build native apps for the platform at launch.

title: Epic Games sues Apple over App Store payment rules
domain_context: Apple's App Store terms require all digital in-app purchases to use Apple's own payment system, on which Apple collects a 15-30% commission, and historically prohibited developers from directing users to external payment options.
description: Epic Games added a direct payment option to Fortnite in violation of App Store rules, triggering an immediate removal from the App Store. Epic had prepared litigation and a public campaign in advance, and the resulting antitrust case continued in US courts for years afterward.

title: NASDAQ Benchmark Case Study — Perspective A: Passive Retail Investor
domain_context: This is an illustrative scenario from the classifier's own documentation, not a reported news event: to secure the listing of SpaceX, NASDAQ hypothetically bends its historical listing rules and index-weighting caps, changing the composition of the QQQ index overnight.
description: NASDAQ adjusts its listing rules and index-weighting caps to accommodate the listing of SpaceX, a large private company, changing what the QQQ index tracks without advance notice to index-fund investors.
source: prompts/Company-Level Classifier.md (Benchmark Case Study, Perspective A)

title: NASDAQ Benchmark Case Study — Perspective B: SpaceX Leadership
domain_context: This is an illustrative scenario from the classifier's own documentation, not a reported news event: SpaceX leadership spent months in closed-door negotiations with NASDAQ, leveraging their private valuation to secure a listing under adjusted rules.
description: SpaceX leadership negotiates with NASDAQ over several months to secure a listing that requires NASDAQ to adjust its historical listing rules and index-weighting caps.
source: prompts/Company-Level Classifier.md (Benchmark Case Study, Perspective B)
